behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff/orchestration (dispatcher -> three reviewer invokers with role-specific payloads). A unit test of one helper can miss wiring mistakes across role routing. Real OS/network/subprocess is not required, and correctness does not depend on model output quality; stub reviewers are sufficient. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about request construction/dispatch correctness, not live model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire dispatcher with in-process stub reviewer clients for extractor/risk/compliance; dispatch one contract; assert each stub received identical source document plus its own expected instruction pack and role tag.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly validates live extraction quality/content from an AI reviewer. Stubs would hide failures in model interpretation and recall. Single model decision step, so Workflow is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real clause extractor on a contract fixture with known key terms; assert extracted parties/dates/obligations/penalties meet acceptance thresholds (allowing bounded output variance).

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: This is plumbing correctness at module boundary (policy config propagation into reviewer call). In-process stubs can catch wrong/missing fields. No real infra needed; no dependence on model judgment content. Rejected higher levels: text checks parameter passing, not whether model policy reasoning is good.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke orchestration with a non-default risk policy and a stub risk-assessor client; assert outbound invocation contains exact policy payload/version and unchanged source document.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on semantic judgment by the live model, especially indirect/euphemistic language detection. Mocked outputs would always pass and hide real failures. Single reviewer quality check, not multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Call real risk assessor with policy and contracts containing explicit + euphemistic risky language; assert returned findings include those clauses with policy-grounded risk labels at agreed quality threshold.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in cross-module request assembly/routing (context propagation to compliance reviewer). A stub compliance client can verify payload fidelity. No real OS/network requirement and no model-output-quality assertion. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is parameter passing only.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with specific jurisdiction + regulatory profile and stub compliance client; assert invocation contains exact jurisdiction/profile fields and expected document payload.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: Correctness is the live AI compliance decision itself (classification against requirements), which is model-output dependent and potentially variable. Stubs cannot validate true decision quality. Single agentic stage, so not Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real compliance checker on contracts with known pass/fail outcomes for configured regulations; assert decision and cited requirement coverage meet acceptance criteria.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments, the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is deterministic merge logic for conflict representation/attribution, which can be exercised in one local merge function/object with crafted inputs. No I/O, no real infra, no model judgment required. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is merge-step conflict handling semantics, not dispatch/network/live-model behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Call merge function with synthetic extractor/risk outputs for same clause with conflicting labels; assert merged report retains both assessments and correct reviewer attribution metadata.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: This is a full multi-step pipeline with multiple agentic reviewer calls and downstream merge/handoff correctness. Failures can emerge from interaction between sequential/parallel agentic stages and aggregation. Lower levels miss cross-step loss/coordination defects.
behaviors.test_description: Run full pipeline on a real contract through live extractor, live risk assessor, and live compliance checker; verify unified report contains all reviewer findings, includes conflict handling, and no stage output is dropped during orchestration/collection.
